---
title: "5.1-Convince-censoring_information"
author: "Sanne Roos"
format: docx
execute:
    echo: false
editor: source
---

```{r set-up}

#0. set-up ----
#load packages
pacman::p_load ("rio",     #to recognize other files and load them in R
                "conflicted", #if a function is in more packages it makes you choose which one to use
                "tidyverse", #tidy data
                "ggplot2",#make plots
                "knitr"
)

#resolve package conflicts
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter) #between dplyr and stats
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::lag) #between dplyr and stats

#set working directory
path <- "/Users/sroos6/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UMCUtrecht/Documenten/Projecten/10-2025 HDF pooling trials/"
#load data
load(paste0(path, "imputed_data_convince.Rdata"))

#rename 
data_all <- dat_imputed

#unimputed in seperate dataset
dat_unimputed <- data_all %>%
    filter(.imp == 0)

#1.0 describe censoring visits for supplemental Table 1----
table_censor <- dat_unimputed %>%
    group_by(id) %>%
    #filter the last row number of each patient
    filter(row_number() == n()) %>% #if the row number is the total amount of row numbers, this is the last row number
               ungroup() %>%
               mutate(
                   cens_reason = case_when(
                       end_eos_reason_recode_full == "Patient died" ~ end_eos_reason_recode_full,
                       .default = end_eot_reason_recode_full
               )
               )
#summarise events per visit and group by censor reason
reason_counts <- table_censor %>%
    group_by(visit, cens_reason) %>%
    summarise(n_reason = n()) %>%
    ungroup()

#get the total events per visit
visit_totals <- reason_counts %>%
    group_by(visit) %>%
    summarise(n_total = sum(n_reason)) %>%
    ungroup()

#left joint these to one table
final_table <- reason_counts %>%
    left_join(visit_totals, by = "visit") %>%
    #begin with highest amount of "outflow" so sort descending
    arrange(visit, desc(n_reason)) %>%
    #duplicated returns true or false for rows who have the same value twice
    #so if there are rows visit 0 visit 0 visit 0, now in will only show visit 0 as text in the first
    mutate(
        visit_label = if_else(duplicated(visit), "", paste0("Visit ", visit)),
        total_label = if_else(duplicated(visit), "", as.character(n_total))
    ) %>%
    select(
        Visit = visit_label,
        `Total per visit` = total_label,
        Reason = cens_reason,
        n = (n_reason)
    )
    

knitr::kable(final_table)
```

"knitr"
